project_type: Next.js 15 App Router grocery delivery PWA with Prisma ORM
build_system: npm with Next.js CLI, Prisma generate required before build
test_framework: Node.js built-in test runner via tsx (tests/*.test.ts), Playwright for E2E
build_command: npm run build (requires DATABASE_URL env var)
verification_instructions: 1. Run npx eslint . to check lint. 2. Run npm run test to check unit tests. 3. Build requires DATABASE_URL so lint + tests are the primary local verification.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET mode. No new npm packages allowed per user instruction. Razorpay and PostHog must use dynamic script loading. DATABASE_URL required for build but not for lint/test.
contribution_requirements: Use Zod for all API validation. Follow feature flag pattern from lib/feature-flags.ts. All new flags added to prisma/feature-flags.ts. Use existing patterns: toast, haptic, motion springs. Feature-flagged features must gracefully degrade when disabled.
key_patterns: Feature flags: prisma/feature-flags.ts defines flags, lib/feature-flags.ts exports isFeatureEnabled()/getFeatureFlag(). API routes use Zod validation, auth via auth(), rate limiting. Side effects are fire-and-forget with Promise.allSettled. Components use framer-motion with springs from lib/motion.ts. Client components marked with 'use client'. Tests use node:test + node:assert/strict.
directory_structure: apps/web/ is the main app. Key dirs: app/ (App Router pages/API), components/ (React components), lib/ (utilities/services), prisma/ (schema + seeds), tests/ (unit tests). Components organized by feature: checkout/, tracking/, admin/, search/, cart/, home/, offers/.
